title: Converting between degrees and radians
problem: Convert (a) $150°$ to radians and (b) $\dfrac{7\pi}{6}$ radians to degrees.

steps.instruction: To convert degrees to radians, multiply by $\dfrac{\pi}{180°}$.
To convert radians to degrees, multiply by $\dfrac{180°}{\pi}$.

title: Finding arc length and sector area
problem: A sector has radius $9$ cm and central angle $\dfrac{5\pi}{6}$ radians. Find (a) the arc length and (b) the area of the sector. Give answers to 2 decimal places.

steps.instruction: Apply the arc length formula $l = r\theta$ with $r = 9$ and $\theta = \dfrac{5\pi}{6}$.
Apply the sector area formula $A = \dfrac{1}{2}r^2\theta$ with $r = 9$ and $\theta = \dfrac{5\pi}{6}$.

answer: Arc length $= \dfrac{15\pi}{2} \approx 23.56$ cm; Sector area $= \dfrac{135\pi}{4} \approx 106.03$ cm²

title: Segment area and arc length — composite problem
problem: A circle has radius $7$ cm. A chord subtends a central angle of $\dfrac{\pi}{2}$ radians. Find (a) the area of the minor segment and (b) the arc length of the minor arc. Give answers to 2 decimal places.

steps.instruction: Subtract to find the segment area: segment $=$ sector $-$ triangle.
Find the arc length using $l = r\theta$.

answer: Segment area $= \dfrac{49(\pi-2)}{4} \approx 13.98$ cm²; Arc length $= \dfrac{7\pi}{2} \approx 11.00$ cm
